title: equisetum × litorale f. arvensiforme
intro: Equisetum × litorale f. arvensiforme, also known as Marsh Horsetail, is a type of horsetail plant that is a hybrid of the equisetum litorale and equisetum arvense species. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and is used to treat various health issues such as urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Marsh Horsetail include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve kidney function, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Marsh Horsetail can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and poultices, which are typically made from the plant's roots and rhizomes.
